# hcifootprint — LLM-facing API surface

> Agents and humans: this single page is meant to be read INSTEAD of the source. It is
> verified against the code; where a claim cites a source file, that file is the authority.
> Package: `hcifootprint`. Beta, pre-1.0. Zero-dependency core.

## What this is

hcifootprint turns a web app's interaction surface into a typed, traversable **skill graph**
an LLM agent can plan over and act on — as the signed-in user, through the app's own buttons
and handlers. You author a navigation tree in your own vocabulary (pages, containers, tools);
the library derives the agent-facing skill graph, gates every action on a serializable guard,
and records each occurrence in a footprintjs commit log. Nothing is hidden silently: anything
the runtime derives rather than observes is stamped with an honesty marker, and every refused
action returns a typed reason. It is not tied to any agent framework — you get a fixed
MCP-shaped tool surface any host can drive.

---

## 1. Authoring — `buildNavigationGraph` (canonical, D18)

`buildNavigationGraph(id, def)` validates and freezes one object literal in a single call (no
`.build()`). Node names in the literal become a typed path union, so a typo in a later
`registerToolGroup('catalog.filtr-rail')` is a COMPILE error, not a silent no-op.

Source: `src/tree/appmap.ts`, `src/tree/types.ts`.

```ts
import { buildNavigationGraph } from 'hcifootprint';

const graph = buildNavigationGraph('shop', {
  pages: {
    catalog: {
      tools: {
        'search':      { does: 'Search dresses by name or color' },
        'add-to-cart': { does: 'Add the open dress to the cart', when: { authenticated: { eq: true } } },
      },
    },
    checkout: {
      modals: {
        'confirm-order': { tools: { 'place-order': { does: 'Place the order', confirm: true } } },
      },
    },
  },
  skills: {
    purchase: { does: 'Buy a dress end to end', steps: ['add-to-cart', 'place-order'] },
  },
});
```

Authoring vocabulary (all optional except where noted):

- `pages` (REQUIRED) — top-level route targets. Each page is a container node.
- Container buckets inside any node: `areas` (coexist, AND), `tabs` (at most one shown),
  `modals` (overlay; a shown blocking modal masks sibling tools — `blocks: false` = popover).
- `tools` — leaf actions on a node. A tool's fields (source: `ToolDef`, `src/tree/types.ts`):
  - `does` (REQUIRED) — one authored sentence; it is BOTH your label and the tool description
    the LLM reads. It is a source-code literal, never runtime text (injection firewall).
  - `when` — availability guard (a flat WhereFilter; see §5). AND-composed with every ancestor `when`.
  - `writes` — state keys this tool claims to change (verified at settlement).
  - `goTo` — page id this tool claims to navigate to.
  - `confirm` — high-effect gate: requires explicit confirmation before firing.
  - `input` — payload contract (Zod, JSON Schema, or any `.safeParse`/`.parse` validator).
  - `binding` — how to reach it on screen (ARIA role + name first); optional (handlers don't need it).
- Root-level `tools` (multi-attach): a tool offered on several pages, each entry needs `on: string | string[]`.
- `skills` — named multi-step flows. Fields (source: `SkillDef2`): `does` (REQUIRED planner text),
  `steps` (REQUIRED, tool ids by qualified path or unambiguous suffix), `when` (precondition guard).
- `repeats: true` on a container = a template (one parameterized tool for N cards). Optional
  `instances: (state) => string[]` is the complete instance set; without it, served lists fall
  back to the mounted window (marked `enumeration: 'mounted-window'`).

Returns a `NavigationGraph` (source: `src/tree/types.ts`): `{ id, spec, nodes, toolNodes,
createSession(opts?), requiredStateKeys() }`. All referential/shape mistakes (unknown `goTo`,
guard-operator typos, ambiguous skill steps, contradictory ancestor/descendant guards) throw at
build time.

**v1 `skillGraph()` is legacy sugar.** The fluent `skillGraph('id').page(...).affordance(...).build()`
builder (source: `src/graph/builder.ts`) still ships and works, but `buildNavigationGraph` is the
canonical authoring surface — it adds the container tree (modals/tabs/repeats), typed node paths,
and mount-time tool declaration. Prefer it for new work.

---

## 2. Session lifecycle

`graph.createSession(opts?)` returns an `InteractionSession`. Source: `src/traverse/nav-session.ts`
(runtime), `src/traverse/session.ts` (base), `src/atom/types.ts` (result types).

### `createSession(opts)` — `InteractionSessionOptions`

Extends the base `SessionOptions` (source: `src/atom/types.ts`) plus two tree-only fields.

- `node?: string` — starting page id. Default: the first declared page.
- `state?: Record<string, unknown>` — initial projected state (the lean snapshot guards read —
  not your whole app store).
- `stateTap?: boolean` — whether this session receives `updateState()` reports. Default: true when
  `state` was provided, else false. Without a tap, declared-writes fires settle on handler
  completion (or immediately) with `effectVerified: 'unobservable'` instead of pending forever.
- `redactedKeys?: string[]` — keys stored as `'REDACTED'` in the commit log; live state keeps raw values.
- `commitValues?: 'full' | 'delta'` — commit-log value encoding (footprintjs dial). Default `'delta'`.
- `onWarn?: (message: string) => void` — dev-warning sink (StrictMode re-registrations, handler
  errors, sensor drift). Default `console.warn`.
- `captureProduced?: boolean` — capture each handler's RETURN value onto its transition
  (`produced`, sanitized + capped) as the "act → get data back" channel. Default true.
- `dormantGraceMs?: number` — how long a registration outside the router-confirmed page may
  persist before drift telemetry fires. Default 3000ms.
- `now?: () => number` — the clock the grace timers read (epoch ms). Default `Date.now`. Inject a
  controllable clock for deterministic staleness tests.

### `registerToolGroup(path, opts?)` — `RegisterToolGroupOptions`

Call when a component that renders `path` mounts. You NEVER name a group — this returns a
`ToolGroupHandle` that IS the identity; hold it and call `.unregister()` on unmount. `path` is
typed against the graph's node paths. Source: `src/traverse/nav-session.ts`.

Options:

- `handlers?: Record<string, ToolHandler>` — bind the app's EXISTING functions (by reference) to
  tools declared on this node. `ToolHandler = (payload?) => unknown | Promise<unknown>`.
- `tools?: Record<string, RegisteredToolDef>` — declare NEW leaf tools here-and-now (the
  register-with-just-a-description path). `RegisteredToolDef` = a `ToolDef` plus an optional `handler`.
- `instance?: string` — instance key when registering one card of a `repeats` container (e.g. `'o-123'`).
- `visible?: boolean` — initial visibility signal (same wire as `setVisible`).
- `enabled?: Record<string, boolean>` — initial per-tool enabled state (grey a button out; flip
  later via `handle.setEnabled(toolId, false)`).

Returns `ToolGroupHandle`: `{ id, node, instance?, setEnabled(toolId, enabled), unregister() }`.
`registerTool(path, toolId, { handler, ... })` is the single-tool convenience form.

### Reporting reality — fire / updateState / sync / stimulus

- `fire(affordanceId, opts)` — apply a transition. `opts` (`FireOptions`): `source` (Principal:
  `'user' | 'agent' | 'system' | 'unknown'`), `payload?`, `instance?`, `expectedVersion?`
  (optimistic-concurrency token from `available().version`), `invoke?` (default true; false =
  record-only, for a DOM sensor). Returns `FireResult` — `{ ok: true, transition, version,
  settlement: 'settled' | 'awaiting-state' }` or a typed `{ ok: false, reason, ... }` (see §6).
- `updateState(delta, opts?)` — the app reports a projected-state delta (router/store tap); pending
  fires settle. `opts` (`UpdateOptions`): `transitionId?` (settle THIS pending precisely — preferred
  over FIFO), `stimulus?`, `principal?`. Returns `UpdateResult`.
- `sync(observedNode, opts?)` — reconcile the cursor when the world moved without an offered edge
  (back button, redirect, deep link). `opts`: `stimulus?`, `principal?`. An unauthored page is not an
  error — the cursor follows reality and `available()` honestly serves zero edges there
  (`offGraph: true`).
- **Stimulus** = world-initiated motion (`StimulusKind`: `'navigation' | 'timeout' | 'push' |
  'structure-swap' | 'unknown'`). It is not a standalone method on the session — you record it via
  the `stimulus` option on `updateState`/`sync`. When set, the delta is NEVER attributed to a pending
  fired transition (a server push must not hijack an action's provenance). (`hcifootprint/testing`'s
  harness exposes a convenience `stimulus()` method — source: `src/testing/harness.ts`.)

### Reading + observing

- `available()` → `AvailableSlice` `{ version, node, edges }`. Each edge carries `affordanceId`,
  `description`, `role`, `highEffect`, guard `evidence`, and honesty stamps: `guardUnevaluated?`,
  `activation?`, `presence?: 'unknown'`, `materialized?`, `enabled?`, `instances?`/`enumeration?`.
- `availableSkills()`, `contextBrief(opts?)` (token-lean prompt context, authored strings only),
  `explain(id)`, `why(key)` (footprint backward causal slice).
- Passive observers: `on('transition' | 'state' | 'structure' | 'gap', listener)` → unsubscribe fn.
  A throwing listener is isolated, never aborts the session.
- Gap ledger: `gaps()` / `onGap(row => ...)` — every refused fire and every explicit `reportGap(...)`
  becomes a token-lean, name-only row for demand-driven triage.

---

## 3. Mode B serving — `skillsAsTools`

`skillsAsTools(session, opts?)` projects the session as a FIXED set of MCP-shaped tools whose bytes
never change for the life of a conversation (keeps the prompt cache warm). Disclosure rides the
RESULT channel. Source: `src/serve/modes.ts`.

```ts
import { skillsAsTools } from 'hcifootprint';

const port = skillsAsTools(session);            // opts: { confirmHighEffect?: boolean=true, source?: Principal='agent' }
const tools = port.tools();                     // static MCPToolDescription[] — register with your host
const result = port.call(name, args);           // route a tool_use; return `result` as the tool_result
```

`tools()` returns, for graph id `shop`:

- one tool per skill: `shop.skill.<id>` (input: `{ step?, input?, confirm?, instance? }`)
- `shop.whats_here` — describe the current position. Optional `{ sinceVersion }`: pass a version
  from any previous result and the reply narrates only the DELTA since it (who did what since the
  model's last look) — cheaper re-grounding after write-steps.
- `shop.do_action` — perform one available action outside any skill flow (`{ action, input?, confirm?, instance? }`)

`MCPToolDescription` = `{ name, description, inputSchema }` (source: footprintjs).

### The three call shapes

- **`shop.whats_here` →** `{ ok: true, brief, actions: [{ action, does, highEffect?, guardUnevaluated?,
  instances?, enumeration?, activation? }], skills: [{ skill, does, feasible, feasibilityUnknownFor? }],
  youAreOn, version }`.
- **`shop.skill.<id>` with no `step` →** opens the skill frame and returns `frameData`:
  `{ ok: true, skill, frame: 'open', judgment, readySteps: [...], laterSteps: [...], howToAct,
  awaitingState?, youAreOn, version }`.
- **`shop.skill.<id>` with `step` →** fires that step: merges `frameData` + `fireData` + position.
- **`shop.do_action` with `action` →** fires one action: `fireData` + position.

### `ServeResult` shape

`ServeResult = Record<string, unknown>` — a plain data object; serialize it as the tool_result body.
Text fields are authored strings only; runtime values (state, payloads, instance keys, evidence,
produced data) are structured DATA fields. Fields you will encounter (source: `src/serve/modes.ts`):

- `ok: boolean` and `judgment` — the routing discriminant on `ok: false`:
  `'blocked'` (skill precondition fails), `'error'` (unknown step/action, bad tool), `'needs-confirm'`
  (high-effect step awaiting `confirm: true`), `'rejected'` (fire refused — carries `reason`).
  On an open frame `judgment` is `'navigate-or-wait' | 'one-ready-step' | 'needs-choice'`; on
  completion `'done'`.
- Position (always): `youAreOn` (current node), `version` (the total-order cursor).
- Frame: `frame` (`'open' | 'completed' | 'cancelled' | 'demoted'`), `readySteps`
  (`[{ step, does, highEffect?, guardUnevaluated?, expects? }]`), `laterSteps` (`[{ step, status }]`),
  `awaitingState?` (steps whose fire has not yet been reported back), `howToAct`.
- Fire success: `did` (the step id fired), `settlement` (`'settled' | 'awaiting-state'`),
  `transitionId` (fetch `producedFor(id)` after awaiting an async handler), `guardUnevaluated?`.
- Fire rejection: `reason` (see §6), plus `evidence?` / `issues?` / `instances?` / `node?` /
  `expects?` / `retriable?` depending on the reason.

---

## 4. THE GUARD RULES (read before writing a state projector)

Source: `src/graph/guards.ts` (shape + operators), `src/traverse/session.ts` `#evalGuard`
(runtime evaluation).

**A guard is a flat WhereFilter.** `key → { op: value }`. Operators (the ONLY eight):
`eq, ne, gt, gte, lt, lte, in, notIn` (`in`/`notIn` take an array). The map is evaluated
**AND-only**: every key must match, and multiple ops under one key must ALL hold. There is
**NO `$or`, no nesting, no disjunction of any kind** — the filter is deliberately flat. `{}` (an
empty guard) is rejected at build time because the evaluator never matches an empty filter.

**Absent guard key ⇒ SERVED, not hidden.** At evaluation, keys of the guard that are NOT present in
the projected state are collected as *unevaluable*. Only the evaluable subset decides `matched`. So:

- a guard key **absent** from projected state → the edge is served ANYWAY, carrying the
  `guardUnevaluated: [key, ...]` marker (the condition is flagged as taken-on-faith — the app stays
  the enforcer). It is NOT treated as false, and NOT hidden.
- a guard key **present but failing** its operator → the edge IS hidden (`available()` omits it).
- `undefined` is never stored: a report entry whose value is `undefined` is dropped (a declared
  write reported as `undefined` counts as MISSING), and a key somehow holding `undefined` is as
  unevaluable as an absent one — it rides `guardUnevaluated`, never sneaks past an operator
  (src/traverse/session.ts).

The consequence: an incompletely-seeded projector silently degrades honest availability into
unevaluated-guess territory (everything shows up flagged instead of actually decided).

**Fix: seed every key from `graph.requiredStateKeys()` (see §7).** A projection covering that whole
set is what lets guards actually decide rather than defer.

**The OR pattern — derive a boolean in your projector.** Because the filter is AND-only, you express
disjunction by computing a derived flag in the state you report, then guarding on that flag:

```ts
// "checkout is allowed when the cart has items OR a saved order exists" — AND-only can't express OR,
session.updateState({ canCheckout: cart.length > 0 || savedOrder != null });   // so derive the boolean
// then author the guard as:  when: { canCheckout: { eq: true } }
```

---

## 5. THE SETTLEMENT RULE (agents: re-read after every write-step)

Source: `src/traverse/session.ts` (`fire` / `#settle`), `src/serve/modes.ts` (`frameData`).

A step that declares `writes` does not settle synchronously when a state tap is wired. `fire()`
returns **`settlement: 'awaiting-state'`** and the transition sits **pending** until the app reports
the real delta via `updateState(...)`. Until then:

- the fired step is excluded from `readySteps` (advertising it would tell the model to double-fire),
  and it appears in `awaitingState`;
- any downstream step whose guard depends on that write is NOT `ready` yet — it only becomes ready
  after the app reports state back.

**Therefore an agent MUST re-read `whats_here` (or re-open the skill tool) after any write-step** —
the readySteps in the write-step's own result were computed before the app moved, so they are stale
for what comes next. (When there is no state tap, or the step declares no writes, `fire()` returns
`settlement: 'settled'` immediately and no re-read wait applies — but re-reading is always safe.)

---

## 6. Typed rejection reasons

`fire()` / Mode B `judgment: 'rejected'` carry one of (source: `FireResult`, `src/atom/types.ts`):
`UNKNOWN_AFFORDANCE`, `STALE_CURSOR`, `NOT_ON_NODE`, `GUARD_FAILED` (with `evidence`),
`PAYLOAD_INVALID` (with `issues`), `BLOCKED_BY_OVERLAY` (a shown modal masks the node),
`NODE_NOT_VISIBLE`, `STILL_MOUNTING` (RETRIABLE — mounts not arrived yet), `INSTANCE_REQUIRED`,
`INSTANCE_UNKNOWN`, `TOOL_DISABLED` (RETRIABLE — greyed out). Every refused fire is also a gap-ledger
row. High-effect steps return `judgment: 'needs-confirm'` and never auto-cross — ask the human, then
call again with `confirm: true`.

---

## 7. `requiredStateKeys()` — the projector seeding checklist

Present on BOTH graph types (source: `src/graph/builder.ts` for `skillGraph`,
`src/tree/appmap.ts` for `buildNavigationGraph`):

```ts
graph.requiredStateKeys();   // → sorted, deduped string[] of every guard key the graph reads
```

It is the sorted, deduped set of state keys every guard reads — across all tool `when`s,
container-node `when`s (they AND-compose into descendant tools), and skill preconditions — whether
or not the node is currently mounted. Seed each of these keys in your state projector so guards
decide instead of deferring (see §4). This is the one call that closes the guard-honesty loop.

---

## Honesty markers (quick reference)

Everything the runtime DERIVES rather than OBSERVES is flagged, never laundered as fact:
`guardUnevaluated` (guard key absent from state), `activation: 'assumed'` (declared subtree,
nothing registered), `presence: 'unknown'` (ambiguous mounted tabs), `materialized: false`
(declared but no handler wired), `effectVerified: 'unobservable'` (no report to check against),
`enumeration: 'mounted-window'` (instance list is partial). App text and produced data ride a strict
DATA channel; only authored strings reach the planner's instruction channel.
